mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance above zero during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses the 5.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a classification accuracy exceeding 52 percent in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60 percent of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.5 percent monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 58 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and boosts aggregate wage income, driving consumer spending and corporate revenues that produce positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 60-day forward returns stays below 0.01, or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP growth below 1.0 percent combined with declining real personal income signals a broad demand shock that reduces corporate profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below 1.0 percent
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a log-loss improvement over a baseline model in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during periods of stable inflation indicates healthy supply chain throughput and inventory replenishment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during stable inflation periods yield negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of cases.
